observations.evidence: justfile: recipes present default, info, test, lint, verify
README.md: H1 title, description, quick start/getting started/install, usage/examples
asp-targets.toml present; .gitignore present; gaps: asp-lock.json not ignored
gitleaks hook configured; gitleaks local config/ignore present
justfile: lint recipe present; lint config present; hook runs lint yes
justfile: typecheck recipe present; tsconfig present; hook evidence present
justfile: test recipe present; package test present; Go tests not found

F0.rationale: Baseline hygiene remains PM/floor-owned and presence-only; it may seed escalation but cannot satisfy depth axes.

P0.rationale: agent-spaces is the ASP/runtime layer; installed local hook verification is the substrate for every deeper agent-facing guard.

S1.rationale: Harness dispatch, runtime environment, scope identity, and config materialization cross authority boundaries where illegal states should become unrepresentable when expressible.

S1.authorityChannelSplit.rationale: agent-spaces is the N=1 source for authority-channel split evidence through DispatchEnv and disjoint runtime env handling; assessment should record current exercise evidence without making the frontier item required.

S2.rationale: Spaces, commands, harness adapters, and package boundaries repeat enough that blessed shapes and scaffolds materially improve agent construction priors.

S3.rationale: Agents enter through AGENTS.md, package-local routers, and runtime context assembly; disclosure must be reachable and target-specific.

S3.affordanceGating.rationale: Unlike wrkq, agent-spaces dynamically projects context by run mode, path, and environment; this is target-shaping vocabulary, not a schema exception.

S4.rationale: Boundary and manifest checks are verify-gating; every firing diagnostic should carry fix/why/exception/do-not-suppress fields.

S5.rationale: agent-spaces has multiple structural checks; suppression growth must stay ticketed and visible before adding more guards.

S6.rationale: The local hook-enforced verify path is the handoff gate for this local-first repo; cloud publish workflows are not a substitute or requirement.

S7.rationale: Public package surfaces, runtime contract hashes, and generated runtime plans can silently rot without executable sensors.

S7.contractHashClosure.rationale: agent-spaces is the N=1 source for compile/profile/spec/start contract hash closure; assessment should record current exercise evidence while the graduation ladder waits for corroboration elsewhere.

S8.rationale: The repo already carries several verify-gating checks; rule authoring and sunset discipline prevent guard sprawl.

TA.rationale: agent-spaces work already relies on coordinator/architect/implementer/verifier separation; high-risk mutation paths still need explicit authority design.

TA.actuatorSplit.until: T-05439 hrc-runtime actuator or credential split route is designed and owned
TA.actuatorSplit.note: Cross-repo residency is expressed by deferred+until+route at N=2; revisit the vocabulary if a third target hits the same shape.
TA.actuatorSplit.rationale: Credential/actuator separation can be mechanized but partly resides outside agent-spaces.

TB.rationale: Skills and package guidance are active process carriers for this repo.

TB.lifecycleHealth.rationale: Lifecycle telemetry, state, and rollback are the section 10-B frontier refinement; agent-spaces should record evidence, but the frontier row is not required.

TC.rationale: The target already has an enablement changelog; continued assessment value depends on harvesting changes after substantial work.

TD.rationale: A large Bun/TS monorepo benefits from executable entry-point and area explainers computed from live code instead of stale prose.

TD.selfDescribingSurfaceConformance.rationale: agent-spaces ships the asp CLI, and praesidium CLI surface drift was the original coordinator nomination for this axis.

TE.rationale: Harness and runtime changes need acceptance and behavioral specs before claim-scoped evidence routing is considered.

TE.claimScopedEvidenceRouting.rationale: The likely enforcement home is a wrkf closeout workflow rather than a simple commit hook; treat as frontier until the workflow carrier proves usable.

TE.crossHarnessConformanceMatrix.rationale: agent-spaces is the N=1 source for the section 10-J cross-harness conformance matrix; assessment should record current exercise evidence without making it a required row.

TF.rationale: wrkf exists globally, but agent-spaces should prove adoption on real high-risk tasks before TF is treated as exercised.

TF.workflowAdoption.until: T-05438 measures the closeout workflow adoption tail over real high-risk agent-spaces tasks
TF.workflowAdoption.rationale: The target needs live workflow transitions with role bindings, obligations, and evidence before adoption can be scored as exercised.
